baseline_dominance.justification: The construction identifies real gaps (chain aggregation, participant anonymity, chain length hiding) that RFC 8693 + BBS+ cannot natively provide. However, the rfc7662_advocate's Attack 2 demonstrates a commitment-only AS with signed JWT introspection can approximate the auditor-facing semantics. The spiffe_engineer's Attack 2 shows the WIMSE characterization in §8 is a strawman (WIMSE doesn't require AS at audit time). The baseline comparison argues against the weakest deployment of RFC 8693, not the strongest. The genuine residual advantage is narrower than claimed: AS-adversarial scenarios where the operator distrusts the AS. Since the construction never formally states an AS-adversarial threat model in §3, the dominance claim is soft — preferable but not provably impossible for the baseline to approximate.

formal_security_argument.justification: The construction has explicit threat models (CHAIN-NARROW-SOUNDNESS, CHAIN-PRIVACY, SCOPE-COMMITMENT-HIDING), named assumptions (A1-A3), and reduction sketches. However, the cryptographer's attacks expose critical formal gaps: (1) CHAIN-PRIVACY game is parameterized to exclude root-hop distinguishability by forcing identical handshakeScopeCommitment — the game proves something strictly weaker than the stated claim; (2) the unblinded public input handshakeScopeCommitment is invertible in O(256×N) Poseidon2 evaluations, directly contradicting the privacy claim for the root hop; (3) G5 multiplexer constraint for inactive-hop nonce equality is stated in prose but no Circom constraint is shown — chain-length hiding is unverified. The formal games are internally consistent but don't cover the informal claims.

implementability.justification: Construction maps precisely to existing Bolyra primitives: Poseidon2/3, EdDSAPoseidonVerifier on Baby Jubjub, BinaryMerkleRoot(20), PLONK proving system, cumulative-bit encoding, nullifier pattern. Circuit cost is estimated at ~216,105 constraints fitting within 2^18 SRS. Per-hop breakdown is detailed (13,155 constraints/hop). Proving time target <5s PLONK / <2s rapidsnark is within Phase 1 budget for agent-class circuits. The only new primitive is Poseidon3 (standard arity variant). Cost of blinding quantified at 3.2% overhead.

adversarial_survival.justification: Tier 3 found multiple attacks the construction cannot answer: (1) cryptographer's Attack 1 — root-hop deanonymization via unblinded handshakeScopeCommitment is a concrete break of the privacy claim for the whistleblower scenario; (2) spiffe_engineer's Attack 4 — no causal binding between proof and runtime execution means the NCUA scenario is oversold (operator can fabricate post-hoc proofs over fake chains); (3) cu_ciso's Attack 1 — forensic dead-end under GLBA/Part 748 with no selective unblinding mechanism; (4) rfc7662_advocate's Attack 4 — binary search oracle over policyFloor recovers final scope in 6 on-chain calls. These are not mitigated — the construction has no answer to root-hop deanonymization or causal binding.

scenario_fit.justification: The NFCU scenario names a concrete stakeholder (Navy Federal CU, 13M+ members) and regulatory requirement (NCUA examination, GLBA Safeguards Rule). However, multiple attacks demonstrate the scenario is undermined: (1) cu_ciso shows NCUA examiners cannot read the audit artifact and no regulatory guidance accepts ZK proofs in lieu of access logs; (2) the privacy properties obstruct the CU's own incident response under Part 748.0(b); (3) chain-length hiding may violate NCUA AI governance disclosure requirements; (4) no off-chain fallback for L2 outages. The scenario is named and plausible but the construction as written creates regulatory conflicts that make it not clearly load-bearing — it's more hypothetical than deployment-ready.

gaps: Root-hop deanonymization: handshakeScopeCommitment is an unblinded Poseidon2 public input invertible in O(256×N) — must blind the on-chain anchor or narrow the privacy claim to exclude root hop
No causal binding between proof witness and actual runtime execution — operator can fabricate post-hoc proofs over synthetic chains that passed all checks but never governed real execution
CHAIN-PRIVACY game excludes root-hop distinguishability by design (forces identical handshakeScopeCommitment) — formal proof is weaker than informal claim
Binary search oracle: adversary with audit-request capability can vary policyFloor across calls to recover final scope in O(log 64) queries — need policyFloor commitment or rate-limiting
No selective unblinding mechanism for CU's own incident response (GLBA §314.4(f) forensics) or regulatory compulsion
Cross-org scenario unsupported: circuit has single agentRegistryRoot with no multi-domain federation mechanism
G5 multiplexer constraint for inactive-hop nonce equality is prose-only — must show Circom constraint or chain-length hiding is unverified
Baseline comparison strawmans WIMSE (claims AS involvement at audit time which is incorrect) and ignores commitment-only AS deployments of RFC 8693
